prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: Business, Sci/Tech, Sports, World.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: Business, Sci/Tech, Sports, World.

Category definitions:
- Business: Corporate earnings, financial markets, company management, retail/sales, economic policy
- Sci/Tech: Technology products, scientific research, software/hardware, tech companies when focus is on technology itself
- Sports: Athletic competitions, teams, players, sporting events
- World: International affairs, geopolitics, regional news, natural disasters, environmental issues with geographic focus

Decision rules:
- Tech companies (Google, Samsung, eBay/PayPal): classify as Business if focus is on financial performance, leadership changes, or business operations; classify as Sci/Tech only if focus is on technology features or innovation
- E-commerce and online platforms: default to Business unless discussing technical infrastructure
- Environmental/nature topics: classify as World if emphasizing geographic regions or global issues; classify as Sci/Tech only if focused on scientific research methods
- When multiple categories apply, prioritize: 1) Sports if athletics mentioned, 2) World if international/regional angle is primary framing, 3) Business if financial/corporate angle present, 4) Sci/Tech for remaining technology topics

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: Business, Sci/Tech, Sports, World.

Category definitions:
- Business: Corporate earnings, financial markets, mergers/acquisitions, company management, retail/sales, economic policy, IPOs
- Sci/Tech: Technology products and features, scientific research and discoveries, software/hardware innovations, computing, engineering breakthroughs
- Sports: Athletic competitions, teams, players, sporting events, Olympics, tournaments, games
- World: International affairs, geopolitics, regional news, natural disasters, wars, conflicts, diplomacy

Critical decision rules:
- E-commerce platforms (eBay, PayPal, Amazon): classify as Sci/Tech when discussing technical issues, outages, or platform features
- Online shopping and cart abandonment topics: classify as Sci/Tech (focus is on website/platform technology)
- Email standards, spam filtering, digital protocols: classify as Sci/Tech (technology infrastructure)
- Wildlife, endangered species, conservation, animal behavior: classify as Sci/Tech unless the primary focus is political/policy responses by governments
- Nature/environmental stories with geographic framing: classify as Sci/Tech if discussing biology, ecology, or species research
- Sports events with international location mentions: classify as Sports (athletics take priority over location)
- Business acquisitions of sports teams: classify as Business only if focused on financial transaction; classify as Sports if discussing team/racing operations
- Tech company news: Business if about financials/leadership; Sci/Tech if about products/technology/technical operations

Priority hierarchy when multiple categories could apply:
1. Sports - if athletic competition or sporting events are the main subject
2. Sci/Tech - if technology systems, scientific research, or species/nature study is the core topic
3. Business - if financial transactions or corporate strategy dominate
4. World - if international politics or regional conflicts are the primary frame

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
